actionable_feedback: Add explicit sheet and cell references (sheet name and cell addresses) in expert_memo.md for the key formula cells used to compute surge margins and scenario results so reviewers can quickly locate the formulas in the workbook.

Include a short worked example inside the workbook (a dedicated sheet) that demonstrates how a remediation delta is applied to a single record step-by-step (show original values, applied delta, formula used, and resulting cached value).

If possible, re-open and save the .xlsx in an independent spreadsheet engine (Excel, LibreOffice) to force recalculation and include either: (a) the recalculated workbook as an additional artifact, or (b) a screenshot or exported CSV of the recalculated results. If you cannot run a spreadsheet in the runtime, include a small reproducible script (python snippet) that recomputes the workbook outputs and the generated CSV so reviewers can re-run locally.

In the workbook, add a lightweight 'validation' sheet that lists the key formula expressions (as text) alongside their cached values and the memo's cited per-record results to make cross-checking trivial.
Clarify in the memo whether remediation deltas are intended to apply globally to all records or only to the trigger operating point, and update the workbook's scenario logic accordingly.
Flag the deliverable for a senior mechanical/turbomachinery engineer review prior to any operational decision-making.
artifact_specific_comments: expert_memo.md: Good separation of results and recommendation; add explicit sheet/cell references and an explicit worked example reference to the workbook.
compressor_surge_3.xlsx: Structurally valid OOXML with formulas and cached values; include a validation sheet and worked example sheet to improve reviewer ergonomics.
feedback_summary: The baseline attempt meets the rubric and was awarded full credit. Deliverables are present, calculations are documented, sensitivity checks and assumptions are stated, and the OOXML workbook contains formulas with cached values. Two verification gaps reduce assurance: (1) the .xlsx could not be rendered in the preview environment (openpyxl missing), preventing a visual inspection; (2) the grader did not perform an independent spreadsheet recalculation to re-run formulas in a spreadsheet engine. Minor auditability improvements were suggested (explicit sheet/cell references in memo and a worked example in the workbook). Recommend implementing those local fixes and, if feasible, performing an independent recalculation or attaching evidence (screenshot/export) of the workbook opened in a spreadsheet engine. Also recommend an optional domain-expert review for high-stakes decisions.

extra_model_fields.notes: No rubric failures recorded; suggested changes are auditability and assurance improvements rather than correctness fixes.

quick_actions_estimate.produce_recalculation_evidence: depends on environment; 10-30 minutes if spreadsheet engine available

recommended_reviewers: technical gate reviewer familiar with surge margin definitions
